zh.lifeSummary: 當William Richard de Venables of Bollen於1376年4月13日出生在英國英格蘭爾波利 費時，他的父親Richard Venables, Knight46歲，母親Joan Thornton Fitton31歲。他於1391年11月8日與Johanna Joan de Massey在英國英格蘭爾伯頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1459年在他的家鄉享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Venables
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Venables (Eure), perhaps named with Late Latin venabulum ‘hunting ground’ (a derivative of venari ‘to hunt’).

variants.name: Venable
meanings: History: American bearers of this name are descended from Abraham Venables, who came to VA from England in or before 1687.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Bollen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Dorset and Wiltshire): variant of   Bolling   2.

variants.name: Bolling
meanings: English: variant of   Bowling  , a habitational name from Bowling in Bradford, Yorkshire.
English: perhaps a nickname for a large person, from Middle English bolling ‘something swollen’, a derivative of Middle English bollen ‘to swell, bulge, puff up’.
German (Bölling): derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Boll 2).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When William Richard de Venables of Bollen was born on 13 April 1376, in Bollin Fee, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Venables, Knight, was 46 and his mother, Joan Thornton Fitton, was 31. He married Johanna Joan de Massey on 8 November 1391, in Burton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1459, in his hometown, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Richard de Venables of Bollen est né le 13 avril 1376, à Bollin Fee, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Venables, Knight, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Joan Thornton Fitton, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Johanna Joan de Massey le 8 novembre 1391, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1459, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: William Richard de Venables of Bollenが1376年4月13日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアボリン フイーで生まれた時，父Richard Venables, Knightは46歳，母Joan Thornton Fittonは31歳であった。1391年11月8日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンでJohanna Joan de Masseyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1459年に故郷で83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Richard de Venables of Bollen(이)가 1376년 4월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 볼린 프이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Venables, Knight(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Thornton Fitton(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1391년 11월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버턴에서 Johanna Joan de Massey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1459년 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Richard de Venables of Bollen nasceu em 13 de abril de 1376, em Bollin Fee, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Venables, Knight, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Joan Thornton Fitton, tinha 31 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Richard de Venables of Bollen родился 13 апреля 1376 г. в местности боллинь фи, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Venables, Knight было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Thornton Fitton, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna Joan de Massey 8 ноября 1391 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1459 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет.
